tldr: Phosphor Icons and Feather Icons are both open-source SVG icon sets, so migration is a rename-and-reimport job. The main caveats are Feather's smaller catalog (~280 icons) and single weight, which may force you to find replacements or keep Phosphor for missing icons.
why_switch: Smaller bundle footprint with simpler SVG output
Reduce design surface area to one consistent style
MIT-licensed open-source with no commercial strings
what_to_export: Inventory of every Phosphor icon name used
Size, color, and className props per usage
Framework package in use (react, vue, flutter, web)
Design token mapping from token name to Phosphor icon
Accessibility labels associated with each icon
List of duotone or fill icons that have no Feather equivalent
Tests or snapshots referencing Phosphor component names
export_method: Both libraries are static, so export is a code inventory. Grep for imports from @phosphor-icons/react, @phosphor-icons/vue, phosphor-flutter, or the web package, and record file path, icon name, and props (size, color, weight, className). Group by weight variant — non-regular weights are the riskiest because Feather offers only one weight. Cross-reference each icon against Feather's published list (currently ~280 icons) and mark misses. Misses split into three buckets: (1) close visual analog exists, (2) no analog and the icon is critical, (3) no analog and the icon can be removed or redesigned. Capture before-state screenshots of representative screens for visual regression. If you have a design-system token map pointing to Phosphor names, export it so reassignment can happen in one PR.
import_method: Install the appropriate Feather package (feather-icons for vanilla, react-feather for React, vue-feather for Vue, etc., depending on what your framework community maintains). Build a Phosphor-to-Feather rename map for icons that exist on both sides. For the misses identified during export, decide per-icon whether to (a) keep Phosphor installed for that single icon, (b) substitute a close Feather analog, or (c) remove the icon. Replace imports module-by-module and update prop names — Feather typically takes size, color, and stroke-width as props. Drop any weight-related logic since Feather is single-weight. Run visual regression and confirm aria-labels remain on the wrapping element.

concept_mapping.note: Feather is single-weight; map to a stroke value
Build a rename map; not all overlap
Feather has no equivalent — substitute or keep Phosphor

gotchas: Feather has ~280 icons vs Phosphor's 1200+; expect missing icons that need substitutes or fallbacks
Single weight only — duotone and fill variants must be redesigned or kept on Phosphor
Feather maintenance has been slow; verify the package version you adopt is stable
Icon names do not all match — manual rename map required
Visual style is more minimal; QA against design tokens to keep contrast and weight balanced
Tests and snapshots referencing Phosphor component names will need updating

email.subject: Migrating icon library from Phosphor Icons to Feather Icons
email.body:
Hi team,

We are planning to migrate our icon library from Phosphor Icons to Feather Icons over the next 14 days. The goal is a more minimal, single-weight aesthetic and a smaller surface area to maintain.

The main risk is Feather's smaller catalog and single weight: any duotone or fill icons we use will need a substitute, and a few icons may not have a Feather analog. We will inventory current usage, build a rename map, and decide per-miss whether to substitute, redesign, or keep Phosphor installed for that single case.

If you own a screen using Phosphor weight variants (bold, duotone, fill) or rely on icons unique to Phosphor, please flag it during the inventory phase. Visual regression will run before merging.

Thanks,
Your customer
